description: Feishu IM channel: MCP stdio bridge (channel.py) and standalone WebSocket message-routing daemon (channel_server.py) for multi-instance customer service

key_symbols.name: channel-instructions
key_symbols.description: Operational instructions injected into MCP server as system prompt — covers mode routing (production/improve/explain), file message handling, available tools (reply, react, plugins), and data paths

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient
key_symbols.description: WebSocket client connecting to channel_server.py with auto-reconnect, message queue, and methods to send replies/reactions/UX events

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient.__init__
key_symbols.description: Initializes with server_url, chat_ids filter, instance_id, runtime_mode, and internal asyncio.Queue

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient.connect
key_symbols.description: Connects to channel-server WebSocket with infinite auto-reconnect loop (3s retry)

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient._register
key_symbols.description: Sends registration message with role/chat_ids/instance_id/runtime_mode to channel-server

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient._message_loop
key_symbols.description: Consumes WebSocket messages: queues type=message, responds to ping, logs errors

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient.send_reply
key_symbols.description: Sends a reply message (chat_id + text) to channel-server via WebSocket

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient.send_react
key_symbols.description: Sends an emoji reaction (message_id + emoji_type) to channel-server via WebSocket

key_symbols.name: ChannelClient.send_ux_event
key_symbols.description: Sends a UX event (chat_id + event + data) to channel-server via WebSocket

key_symbols.name: inject_message
key_symbols.description: Builds a JSONRPCNotification with channel metadata (chat_id, user, runtime_mode, etc.) and sends it into the MCP write_stream as a SessionMessage for Claude Code consumption

key_symbols.name: _load_identity
key_symbols.description: Reads .autoservice/identity.yaml and formats identity preamble (name, description, modes, rules) for MCP server instructions

key_symbols.name: _build_instructions
key_symbols.description: Combines identity preamble + channel-instructions.md into the full MCP server instructions text

key_symbols.name: _refresh_instructions
key_symbols.description: Hot-reloads instructions if channel-instructions.md or identity.yaml file modification time changed

key_symbols.name: create_server
key_symbols.description: Creates and returns an MCP Server('autoservice-channel') with built instructions text

key_symbols.name: register_tools
key_symbols.description: Registers core tools (reply, react) and dynamic plugin tools on the MCP server via list_tools/call_tool handlers

key_symbols.name: _handle_reply
key_symbols.description: Tool handler for 'reply': schedules send_reply coroutine on event loop via run_coroutine_threadsafe

key_symbols.name: _handle_react
key_symbols.description: Tool handler for 'react': schedules send_react coroutine on event loop via run_coroutine_threadsafe

key_symbols.name: main
key_symbols.description: Async entry point: discovers plugins, creates ChannelClient + MCP Server, runs server.run/client.connect/consume_messages in parallel via anyio task group over stdio

key_symbols.name: entry_point
key_symbols.description: Synchronous wrapper calling asyncio.run(main()), used as console_scripts entry point

key_symbols.name: Instance
key_symbols.description: Dataclass representing a registered channel.py or web/app.py WebSocket client with ws, instance_id, role, chat_ids, runtime_mode, business_mode, connected_at

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer
key_symbols.description: Local WebSocket server (default port 9999) that routes messages between Feishu inbound, web clients, and registered Claude Code channel instances using exact/prefix/wildcard routing

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.__init__
key_symbols.description: Initializes host, port, feishu_enabled flag, admin_chat_id, and three route tables: exact_routes, prefix_routes, wildcard_instances

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.start
key_symbols.description: Starts WebSocket server via websockets.serve and optionally launches Feishu WS integration task

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.stop
key_symbols.description: Gracefully shuts down: cancels background tasks, closes WebSocket server, notifies admin

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._load_credentials
key_symbols.description: Static method loading Feishu app_id/app_secret from env vars or .feishu-credentials.json (searches project root and git toplevel)

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._resolve_user
key_symbols.description: Resolves Feishu open_id to display name via cache, Feishu contact API, and CRM upsert_contact

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._send_reaction
key_symbols.description: Adds emoji reaction to a Feishu message via API; optionally tracks reaction_id for later removal

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._remove_reaction
key_symbols.description: Removes a previously tracked ACK reaction from a Feishu message via API

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._download_feishu_file
key_symbols.description: Downloads file/image/audio/media attachments from Feishu API, saves to .autoservice/uploads/{chat_id}/, returns local path

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_admin_message
key_symbols.description: Processes admin slash-commands: /help, /status, /inject #N <text>, /explain <query>

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._reply_feishu
key_symbols.description: Sends text message to a Feishu chat via CreateMessageRequest in a daemon thread (best-effort, fire-and-forget)

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._run_feishu_safe
key_symbols.description: Error-logging wrapper around _run_feishu

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._run_feishu
key_symbols.description: Main Feishu integration: initializes lark Client, sets up P2ImMessageReceiveV1 handler, starts WS in daemon thread, sends startup notifications to scoped users, runs consumer loop routing messages

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_client
key_symbols.description: Handles a single WebSocket client connection: dispatches register/reply/react/message/ux_event/pong message types

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_register
key_symbols.description: Registers an instance into exact_routes/prefix_routes/wildcard_instances, checks for conflicts on exact chat_ids

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._unregister
key_symbols.description: Removes a disconnected instance from all route tables

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.route_message
key_symbols.description: Three-tier routing: exact match -> prefix match -> wildcard broadcast; adds routed_to hint for observation mode

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_reply
key_symbols.description: Reverse-routes replies: Feishu chats (oc_*) go to Feishu API + remove ACK reaction; web chats (web_*) relayed to web instance

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_react
key_symbols.description: Forwards reaction requests to Feishu API in a daemon thread

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_inbound_message
key_symbols.description: Handles inbound messages from web clients, tracks in _known_chats, routes via route_message

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._handle_ux_event
key_symbols.description: Forwards UX events to the appropriate web connection via exact/prefix route lookup

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._chat_index
key_symbols.description: Returns numbered list of (index, chat_id, info) for active chats used by /status and /inject

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._resolve_chat_target
key_symbols.description: Resolves /inject target — accepts #N index or full chat_id string

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.status_text
key_symbols.description: Generates human-readable status report: message stats, service desks, channel status, active conversations

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer.help_text
key_symbols.description: Generates help text listing all admin slash-commands (/status, /help, /inject, /explain)

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._find_prefix_instance
key_symbols.description: Finds the Instance matching a chat_id by prefix pattern

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._send
key_symbols.description: Static helper to send JSON dict over WebSocket with error handling

key_symbols.name: ChannelServer._notify_admin
key_symbols.description: Fire-and-forget admin notification; logs if no admin_chat_id configured

key_symbols.name: _async_main
key_symbols.description: Async entry point: configures logging, reads env vars (CHANNEL_SERVER_PORT, ADMIN_CHAT_ID, FEISHU_ENABLED), creates ChannelServer, registers signal handlers, starts server, prints banner, waits for stop

key_symbols.name: main
key_symbols.description: Synchronous entry point calling asyncio.run(_async_main())

dependencies: lark_oapi (lazy, in channel_server.py for Feishu API)
requests (lazy, in send_startup for HTTP token fetch)

test_results.command: No dedicated test files found for feishu/ module
test_results.note: The feishu module has no test suite. Testing would require mocking the WebSocket layer (websockets), MCP server (mcp.server), and Feishu API (lark_oapi). The module is integration-heavy with no unit tests.

user_flows.name: Feishu message ingestion and routing
steps: 1. channel_server.py starts WebSocket server on port 9999 and connects to Feishu via lark_oapi WS client in daemon thread
2. Feishu WS receives P2ImMessageReceiveV1 event in on_message callback (line 475)
3. on_message filters bot's own messages, deduplicates via _seen set, sends ACK reaction
4. Parses text/post/file/image/audio message types, resolves user name via Feishu API + CRM
5. Handles mode-switch commands (/improve, /production) or enqueues message dict to asyncio.Queue
6. Consumer loop (line 722) reads from queue, calls route_message() for normal messages or _handle_admin_message() for admin slash-commands
7. route_message (line 860) dispatches to exact_routes -> prefix_routes -> wildcard_instances

user_flows.name: MCP channel bridge (Claude Code integration)
steps: 1. channel.py main() discovers plugins via autoservice.plugin_loader.discover (line 322-325)
2. Creates ChannelClient connecting to channel_server at ws://localhost:9999 (line 333)
3. Creates MCP Server with instructions from identity.yaml + channel-instructions.md (line 339)
4. Registers core tools (reply, react) and plugin tools on MCP server (line 340)
5. Opens MCP stdio transport, runs 3 parallel tasks via anyio task group (line 363): server.run, client.connect, consume_messages
6. consume_messages reads from ChannelClient queue, hot-reloads instructions, calls inject_message to push notifications into MCP write_stream
7. inject_message (line 135) builds JSONRPCNotification with method 'notifications/claude/channel' and sends as SessionMessage
8. Claude Code processes the notification, may call reply/react tools which schedule coroutines on the event loop back to ChannelClient

user_flows.name: Reply flow (Claude Code -> Feishu user)
steps: 1. Claude Code calls 'reply' MCP tool with chat_id + text
2. _handle_reply (channel.py:293) schedules ChannelClient.send_reply coroutine via run_coroutine_threadsafe
3. ChannelClient.send_reply (channel.py:106) sends JSON {type: reply, chat_id, text} to channel_server via WebSocket
4. ChannelServer._handle_reply (channel_server.py:905) checks chat_id prefix: oc_ -> Feishu, web_ -> web relay
5. For Feishu: calls _reply_feishu (line 408) which creates CreateMessageRequest in daemon thread
6. Removes ACK reaction from the last inbound message in that chat

user_flows.name: Admin command flow
steps: 1. Admin sends /status, /help, /inject, or /explain in the admin Feishu group chat
2. Consumer loop (line 737) detects admin_chat_id match + slash prefix, calls _handle_admin_message (line 341)
3. /status -> generates status_text() with instance list, channel status, active conversations
4. /inject #N <text> -> resolves chat target via _resolve_chat_target, creates injected message, calls route_message
5. /explain <query> -> creates explain-mode message with runtime_mode=explain and admin_chat_id, routes to instances

user_flows.name: Web client message flow
steps: 1. Web client (web/app.py) connects to channel_server via WebSocket, sends register with chat_ids=[web_*] prefix pattern
2. _handle_register (line 789) stores in prefix_routes as 'web_' -> Instance
3. Web client sends type=message with chat_id=web_xxx, handled by _handle_inbound_message (line 941)
4. Message is tracked in _known_chats and routed via route_message to exact/prefix/wildcard instances
5. Replies from Claude Code with chat_id=web_xxx are reverse-routed to the web Instance in _handle_reply (line 917)

user_flows.name: Instance registration and routing
steps: 1. channel.py connects to channel_server and sends register with role/chat_ids/instance_id (ChannelClient._register, line 82)
2. _handle_register (line 789) validates no exact-route conflicts, creates Instance dataclass
3. Routes stored: '*' -> wildcard_instances, 'prefix*' -> prefix_routes, exact chat_id -> exact_routes
4. On disconnect, _unregister (line 835) cleans up all route table entries
5. route_message (line 860) implements 3-tier priority: exact > prefix > wildcard, with routed_to hints for observation mode
